codeReview.summary: This feature is purely operational — creating beads epics and verifying file paths. No source code was written or modified. The first worker (dbfca972) created 3 beads epics using `bd create --type epic` and verified model paths. The second worker (47eb194a) confirmed the first worker's work was already complete. Both workers ran comprehensive verification: builds (macOS + iOS), lint, format, and tests all pass. The 3 epics (NetMonitor20-q9a Phase 1, NetMonitor20-x47 Phase 2, NetMonitor20-hay Phase 3) have detailed, accurate descriptions that align with the PRD phases. Models are in correct package paths: Models/Heatmap/ (HeatmapEnums.swift, HeatmapModels.swift) and Services/Heatmap/ (HeatmapRenderer.swift, WiFiMeasurementEngine.swift, SurveyFileManager.swift).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The swift-core-worker skill's Step 2 (TDD — Write Tests First) doesn't apply to non-code operational features like creating beads epics and verifying paths. Both workers noted this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges. The skill could include a note: 'Skip TDD for features with no code changes (e.g., issue tracking, path verification, configuration-only tasks).'
sharedStateObservations.evidence: First worker skillFeedback: 'The swift-core-worker skill's TDD step (Step 2) doesn't apply well to features that are purely operational'. Second worker deviation: 'Verified existing work instead of writing new tests' with reason 'Feature was already completed by previous worker'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The swift-core-worker skill has no guidance for when a feature was already completed by a previous worker session. The second worker had to figure out that the work was done and just needed verification. A note like 'If preconditions show work already completed, verify rather than re-implement' would save worker context window.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Second worker (47eb194a) transcript shows it recognized work was done by previous worker (dbfca972) and pivoted to verification mode. Skill feedback suggests: 'Swift-core-worker skill could include a step for if feature already completed by previous worker verify and report to handle re-assigned features'.

summary: Pass. All 3 beads epics created with detailed descriptions matching PRD phases. Epic Phase 1 (NetMonitor20-q9a) covers macOS+iOS blueprint walk survey. Phase 2 (NetMonitor20-x47) covers iOS AR map creation. Phase 3 (NetMonitor20-hay) covers iOS AR continuous scan. Foundation models verified in correct package paths per AGENTS.md placement table. Fulfills VAL-CROSS-007 (build integrity) and VAL-CROSS-008 (lint/format). No code changes, no blocking issues.
